com.hp.workpath.api.helper.email

Class EmailAttributes.Builder

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.helper.email.EmailAttributes.Builder
  • Enclosing class:
    EmailAttributes

    public static class EmailAttributes.Builder
extends java.lang.Object
    Builder for creating EmailAttributes containing email parameters.
    Since:
    API 1

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Builder()
      Default constructor constructs a new Builder with default attributes.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description

      Adds files as email attachments.
      EmailAttributes.Builder addBccAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress, java.lang.String name)
      Adds "Bcc" value to an email message.
      EmailAttributes.Builder addBccAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
      Adds multiple "Bcc" values to an email message.
      EmailAttributes.Builder addCcAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress, java.lang.String name)
      Adds "Cc" value to an email message.
      EmailAttributes.Builder addCcAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
      Adds multiple "Cc" values to an email message.
      EmailAttributes.Builder addToAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress, java.lang.String name)
      Adds "To" value to an email message.
      EmailAttributes.Builder addToAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
      Adds multiple "To" values to an email message.

      Combines all of the attributes in this into a EmailAttributes object.

      Sets "From" for an email message

      Sets text content for an email message.
      EmailAttributes.Builder setSubject(java.lang.String subject)
      Sets Subject for an email message.

    • Constructor Detail

      • Builder

        public Builder()
        Default constructor constructs a new Builder with default attributes.
        Since:
        API 1

    • Method Detail

      • addToAddress

        public EmailAttributes.Builder addToAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress,
                                            java.lang.String name)
        Adds "To" value to an email message. Includes optional "Name" parameter for recipient's display name.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:

        emailAddress - email address of the recipient

        • Value can not be empty. Atlease one recipient address should be added to the list.

        name - name of the recipient

        • Value can be empty , if no name provided for email.

        Returns:
        Builder builder with addToAddress.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(emailAddress/name) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • addToAddresses

        public EmailAttributes.Builder addToAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
        Adds multiple "To" values to an email message.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:

        emailAddresses - array of email addresses.

        • Value can not be empty. Atlease one recipient address should be added to the array.

        Returns:
        Builder builder with addToAddresses.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(emailAddress) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • addCcAddress

        public EmailAttributes.Builder addCcAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress,
                                            java.lang.String name)
        Adds "Cc" value to an email message. Includes optional "Name" parameter for recipient's display name.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:

        emailAddress - email address of the recipient

        • Value can be empty , if no cc email address provided.

        name - name of the recipient

        • Value can be empty , if no name provided for email.

        Returns:
        Builder builder with addCcAddress.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(emailAddress/name) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • addCcAddresses

        public EmailAttributes.Builder addCcAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
        Adds multiple "Cc" values to an email message.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:

        emailAddresses - array of email addresses

        • Value can be empty , if no cc email address provided.

        Returns:
        Builder builder with addCcAddresses.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(emailAddress) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • addBccAddress

        public EmailAttributes.Builder addBccAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress,
                                             java.lang.String name)
        Adds "Bcc" value to an email message. Includes optional "Name" parameter for recipient's display name.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:

        emailAddress - email address of the recipient.

        • Value can be empty , if no bcc email address provided.

        name - name of the recipient.

        • Value can be empty , if no name provided for email.

        Returns:
        Builder builder with addBccAddress.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(emailAddress/name) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • addBccAddresses

        public EmailAttributes.Builder addBccAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
        Adds multiple "Bcc" values to an email message.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:

        emailAddresses - array of email addresses

        • Value can be empty , if no bcc email address provided.

        Returns:
        Builder builder with addBccAddresses.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(emailAddress) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • setFrom

        public EmailAttributes.Builder setFrom(java.lang.String emailAddress,
                                       java.lang.String name)
        Sets "From" for an email message

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:

        emailAddress - email address of the sender.

        • Value can not be empty. Atlease one sender address should be provided.

        name - name of the sender.

        • Value can be empty , if no name provided for sender email address.

        Returns:
        this builder for method chaining
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(emailAddress/name) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • setSubject

        public EmailAttributes.Builder setSubject(java.lang.String subject)
        Sets Subject for an email message.
        Parameters:

        subject - subject for email message.

        • Value can be empty , if no subject provided for email.

        Returns:
        this builder for method chaining.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(subject) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • setMessage

        public EmailAttributes.Builder setMessage(java.lang.String message)
        Sets text content for an email message.
        Parameters:

        message - email message body text.

        • Value can be empty , if no message provided for email body.

        Returns:
        this builder for method chaining.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(message) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • addAttachments

        public EmailAttributes.Builder addAttachments(java.io.File... files)
        Adds files as email attachments.
        Parameters:

        files - array of email attachment files.

        • Value can be empty , if no attachment provided for email.

        Returns:
        Builder builder with attachments.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if calling a method on a null reference(files) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • build

        public EmailAttributes build()
                      throws CapabilitiesExceededException
        Combines all of the attributes in this into a EmailAttributes object.
        Returns:
        a EmailAttributes object containing all of the attributes.
        Throws:
        CapabilitiesExceededException - if attributes are out of supported range.
        Since:
        API 1
